question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted `NftAllowanceRequest` with `order=DESC` and a wide `ownerOrSpenderIds` range to cause the mirror node to present NFT allowance history in reverse chronological order, making recent revocations appear older than grants, thereby reorganizing the visible transaction history for any observer querying the same account?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged user toggle the `isOwner` flag between `true` and `false` in successive requests to produce two contradictory orderings of the same NFT allowance history — one sorted by `SPENDER` and one by `OWNER` — causing downstream consumers to reconstruct a false sequence of allowance grants and revocations?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: checkOwnerSpenderParamValidity()] Can an unprivileged user craft a request where `ownerOrSpenderIds` is empty and `tokenIds` is also empty, bypassing the `token.id parameter must have account.id present` check, and then use the resulting unconstrained query to retrieve a full unfiltered allowance history that exposes the ordering of all NFT allowances across all accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: checkOwnerSpenderParamValidity()] Can an unprivileged user supply `tokenIds` with a `LT` operator and `ownerOrSpenderIds` with only a `GTE` operator — which passes the cardinality check at line 46-48 — to produce a query that skips the lower boundary of the allowance history, effectively hiding early grant records and making a later grant appear to be the first in history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: checkOwnerSpenderParamValidity()] Can an unprivileged user supply `tokenIds` with a `GT` operator and `ownerOrSpenderIds` with only a `LTE` operator — passing the cardinality check at line 50-52 — to produce a query that excludes the upper boundary of the allowance history, hiding the most recent revocation record and making an older state appear current?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: verifyRangeId()] Can an unprivileged user submit a `Bound` with both `GTE` and `LTE` operators set to the same entity ID value, which passes `verifySingleOccurrence()` and `verifyEqualOrRange()`, but causes `adjustLowerRange()` to rewrite the bound to `EQ`, silently collapsing a range query into a point query and hiding all allowance history records outside that single entity ID?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged user set `limit` to `1` and iterate through NFT allowance history one record at a time using cursor-style pagination with `GTE`/`GT` operators, reconstructing the full allowance history in a custom order that differs from the canonical on-chain ordering, effectively reorganizing the history in their own presentation layer?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged user set `limit` to an extremely large value (e.g., `Integer.MAX_VALUE`) in `NftAllowanceRequest` to retrieve the entire NFT allowance history in a single response, bypassing any intended pagination ordering and receiving records in a bulk dump that obscures the true sequential history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged user supply an `accountId` as an EVM address via `EntityIdEvmAddressParameter` that resolves to a different numeric entity ID than expected due to alias/EVM address collision in `EntityServiceImpl.lookup()`, causing the allowance history of a different account to be returned and presented as if it belongs to the queried account, reorganizing the visible history attribution?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged user supply an `accountId` as an alias via `EntityIdAliasParameter` that resolves to a different entity than the numeric ID they intend to query, causing `entityService.lookup()` to return a mismatched `EntityId`, and the resulting `findAll()` call to return allowance history records attributed to the wrong account, reorganizing history attribution?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: checkOwnerSpenderParamValidity()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the condition at line 41-44 where `!ownerOrSpenderParams.hasLowerAndUpper()` is true and `tokenParams.getAdjustedLowerRangeValue() > tokenParams.adjustUpperBound()` is false, to submit a token range that spans the entire ID space, causing the query to return allowance records in an order that skips intermediate history entries?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getBounds()] Can an unprivileged user craft a `NftAllowanceRequest` where `ownerOrSpenderIds` is empty but `tokenIds` is non-empty, causing `getBounds()` to return `List.of(tokenIds)` as the sole primary bound, which then drives the sort order in `findAll()` by `TOKEN_ID` instead of `SPENDER`/`OWNER`, reorganizing the presentation of allowance history by token rather than by account?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `SORT_ORDERS` map in `NftAllowanceRepositoryCustomImpl` by toggling `isOwner` between requests to switch between `(SPENDER.asc(), TOKEN_ID.asc())` and `(OWNER.asc(), TOKEN_ID.asc())` sort orders, producing two different orderings of the same allowance history that can be combined to create a misleading narrative about the sequence of allowance events?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `APPROVAL_CONDITION` (`NFT_ALLOWANCE.APPROVED_FOR_ALL.isTrue()`) filter in `getBaseCondition()` to observe that only `approvedForAll=true` records are returned, and then infer the existence and ordering of revoked allowances (`approvedForAll=false`) by comparing gaps in the returned sequence, effectively reconstructing a reorganized view of the full allowance history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: adjustUpperBound()] Can an unprivileged user supply a `tokenIds` bound with `upper.operator() == LT` and `upper.value() == Long.MIN_VALUE`, causing `adjustUpperBound()` to compute `Long.MIN_VALUE - 1` which overflows to `Long.MAX_VALUE`, making the upper bound wrap around and include all token IDs in the query, returning the entire allowance history in an unintended order?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getAdjustedLowerRangeValue()] Can an unprivileged user supply an `ownerOrSpenderIds` bound with `lower.operator() == GT` and `lower.value() == Long.MAX_VALUE`, causing `getAdjustedLowerRangeValue()` to compute `Long.MAX_VALUE + 1` which overflows to `Long.MIN_VALUE`, making the lower bound wrap around to the minimum value and causing the query to return all allowance history records from the beginning, reorganizing the visible history window?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getInclusiveRangeValue()] Can an unprivileged user supply a `Bound` with `operator == GT` and `value == Long.MAX_VALUE`, causing `getInclusiveRangeValue(false)` to compute `Long.MAX_VALUE + 1` which overflows to `Long.MIN_VALUE`, producing a SQL condition `field = Long.MIN_VALUE` that matches records at the very beginning of the allowance history, reorganizing which records appear in the result set?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getInclusiveRangeValue()] Can an unprivileged user supply a `Bound` with `operator == LT` and `value == Long.MIN_VALUE`, causing `getInclusiveRangeValue(true)` to compute `Long.MIN_VALUE - 1` which overflows to `Long.MAX_VALUE`, producing a SQL condition `field = Long.MAX_VALUE` that matches records at the very end of the allowance history, causing the upper boundary condition to include records that should be excluded?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getBoundConditions()] Can an unprivileged user craft a `NftAllowanceRequest` with both `ownerOrSpenderIds` and `tokenIds` bounds set to overlapping ranges, causing `getBoundConditions()` to generate a complex OR-based SQL condition that includes records outside the intended range due to the recursive lower/middle/upper condition logic, returning allowance history records in an order that skips or duplicates entries?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: adjustUpperRange()] Can an unprivileged user craft a secondary `tokenIds` bound where `lower.operator() == EQ`, causing `adjustUpperRange()` to set `upper = lower`, and then the resulting SQL condition to treat the token ID range as a point query, silently excluding all token IDs above and below that value and reorganizing the visible allowance history to show only a single token's records?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: adjustLowerRange()] Can an unprivileged user craft an `ownerOrSpenderIds` bound where `hasEqualBounds()` returns true (i.e., `GTE X, LTE X`), causing `adjustLowerRange()` to rewrite the bound to `EQ X` and set `upper = null`, which then causes `getPrimaryCondition()` to return `noCondition()` for the primary bound, effectively removing the primary sort constraint and allowing the query to return records in an unintended order?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getMiddleCondition()] Can an unprivileged user craft a request where the primary `ownerOrSpenderIds` bound has `EQ` operator and the secondary `tokenIds` bound also has `EQ` operator, causing `containsEqOperator()` to return true and `getMiddleCondition()` to AND the secondary condition into the middle condition, producing a SQL query that returns records matching both exact values and reorganizing the history to show only that specific (owner/spender, token) pair?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: verifyEqualOrRange()] Can an unprivileged user submit a `Bound` with `EQ` cardinality of 0 and both `GT` and `LT` operators set, which passes `verifyEqualOrRange()` since the EQ cardinality check is 0, and then exploit the resulting open-ended range to retrieve a large window of allowance history records in a custom order that reorganizes the visible history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: verifySingleOccurrence()] Can an unprivileged user submit a `Bound` with one `GT` and one `GTE` operator simultaneously, which causes `verifySingleOccurrence(GT, GTE)` to throw an exception, but by catching this error and iterating through valid combinations, map out the exact boundaries of the allowance history and reconstruct the full ordering of records through error-based enumeration?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: verifyUnsupported()] Can an unprivileged user submit a `Bound` with `NE` operator for `ownerOrSpenderIds`, which triggers `verifyUnsupported(RangeOperator.NE)` to throw `IllegalArgumentException`, and use the timing or content of this error response to infer the existence of specific entity IDs in the allowance history, enabling reconstruction of the history ordering through oracle-based probing?
